revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 椎取神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太宜命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=椎取神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|pos…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 椎取神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太宜命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=椎取神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}} }} '''椎取神社'''（しいとりじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[三宅村]]（[[三宅島]]）にある[[神社]]。
三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]沿いに鎮座している。
[[式内社]]である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目に比定。
== 旧社殿 == 現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、[[三宅島#2000年の噴火|2000年（平成12年）の噴火]]の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。
<gallery> File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)] - 公式サイト * [https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会
{{DEFAULTSORT:しいとりしんしや}} [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:三宅村の建築物]] [[Category:三宅村の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 椎取神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太宜命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=椎取神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}} }} '''椎取神社'''（しいとりじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[三宅村]]（[[三宅島]]）にある[[神社]]。
三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]沿いに鎮座している。
[[式内社]]である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目の子「したい」に比定<ref name="official"/>。
== 旧社殿 == 現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、[[三宅島#2000年の噴火|2000年（平成12年）の噴火]]の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。
<gallery> File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)] - 公式サイト * [https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会
{{DEFAULTSORT:しいとりしんしや}} [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:三宅村の建築物]] [[Category:三宅村の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{Expand Japanese|椎取神社}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Shiitori Shrine
| native_name = 椎取神社
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes
| mapframe-point = none

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 2 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 椎取神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太宜命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=椎取神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}} }} '''椎取神社'''（しいとりじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[三宅村]]（[[三宅島]]）にある[[神社]]。
三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]沿いに鎮座している。
[[式内社]]である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目の子「したい」に比定<ref name="official"/>。
== 旧社殿 == 現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、[[三宅島#2000年の噴火|2000年（平成12年）の噴火]]の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。
<gallery> File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)] - 公式サイト * [https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会
{{DEFAULTSORT:しいとりしんしや}} [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:三宅村の建築物]] [[Category:三宅村の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{translated page|ja|椎取神社|version=102291556|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 椎取神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太宜命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=椎取神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}} }} '''椎取神社'''（しいとりじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[三宅村]]（[[三宅島]]）にある[[神社]]。
三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]沿いに鎮座している。
[[式内社]]である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目の子「したい」に比定<ref name="official"/>。
== 旧社殿 == 現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、[[三宅島#2000年の噴火|2000年（平成12年）の噴火]]の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。
<gallery> File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)] - 公式サイト * [https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会
{{DEFAULTSORT:しいとりしんしや}} [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:三宅村の建築物]] [[Category:三宅村の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]
==End Japanese==

[[式内社]]である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目の子「したい」に比定<ref name="official"/>。
== 旧社殿 == 現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、[[三宅島#2000年の噴火|2000年（平成12年）の噴火]]の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。
<gallery> File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)] - 公式サイト * [https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会
==End Japanese== [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:三宅村の建築物]] [[Category:三宅村の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 椎取神社
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}{{jalink|神着村|Q11590690|神着}}

{{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}
}}
'''椎取神社'''（しいとりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

三宅島北東、周回道路である{{jalink|都道212号}}沿いに鎮座している。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目の子「したい」に比定<ref name="official"/>。

=== 旧社殿 ===
現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、{{jalink|三宅島#2000年の噴火|Q1075076|2000年（平成12年）の噴火}}の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿
</gallery>

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

* [https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 椎取神社
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}{{jalink|神着村|Q11590690|神着}}

{{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}
}}
'''椎取神社'''（しいとりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

三宅島北東、周回道路である{{jalink|都道212号}}沿いに鎮座している。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目の子「したい」に比定<ref name="official"/>。

=== 旧社殿 ===
現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、{{jalink|三宅島#2000年の噴火|Q1075076|2000年（平成12年）の噴火}}の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿
</gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}

'''椎取神社'''（しいとりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

三宅島北東、周回道路である{{jalink|都道212号}}沿いに鎮座している。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目の子「したい」に比定<ref name="official"/>。

=== 旧社殿 ===
現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、{{jalink|三宅島#2000年の噴火|Q1075076|2000年（平成12年）の噴火}}の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿
</gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}

'''椎取神社'''（しいとりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

三宅島北東、周回道路である{{jalink|都道212号}}沿いに鎮座している。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目の子「したい」に比定<ref name="official"/>。

=== 旧社殿 ===
現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、{{jalink|三宅島#2000年の噴火|Q1075076|2000年（平成12年）の噴火}}の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿
</gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

三宅島北東、周回道路である{{jalink|都道212号}}沿いに鎮座している。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''志理太宜神社'''（しりたきじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 志理太宜命（しりたきのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の5番目の子「したい」に比定<ref name="official"/>。

=== 旧社殿 ===
現在の鳥居・社殿の南方至近に旧の鳥居・社殿があるが、{{jalink|三宅島#2000年の噴火|Q1075076|2000年（平成12年）の噴火}}の際に泥流に埋もれてしまったため、その北方至近に現在の鳥居・社殿が建てられた<ref name="kanko"/>。

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|埋もれた「椎取神社」の旧鳥居・社殿
</gallery>

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)] - 公式サイト
* [https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - 三宅島観光協会

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Shiitori Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} on {{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{jalink|Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} on {{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{jalink|Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} on {{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{jalink|Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Shiitori Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} on {{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{jalink|Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

{{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}
}}
'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

{{Reflist}}

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)] - official site
* [https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Shiitori Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}}{{ill|lt=Kamitsuki Village|Q11590690|WD=Q11590690|lt=Kamitsuki}}

{{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Shiitori Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}}{{ill|lt=Kamitsuki Village|Q11590690|WD=Q11590690|lt=Kamitsuki}}

{{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}
}}
'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)|1=Miyake-jima}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)|1=Miyake-jima}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Shiitori Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kamitsuki Village|Q11590690|WD=Q11590690|lt=Kamitsuki|1=Kamitsuki}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)|1=Miyake-jima}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Shiitori Shrine

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)|1=Miyake-jima}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Shiitori Shrine

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)|1=Miyake-jima}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Shiitori Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}

{{coord2|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|26.7|N|139|33|27.8|E}}}}
}}
'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)|1=Miyake-jima}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Shiitori Shrine'''|椎取神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)|1=Miyake-jima}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>

'''Shiitori Shrine''' (しいとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in northeastern Miyake-jima along Metropolitan Route 212 (the island loop road).

It is identified with '''Shiritagi Shrine''' (しりたきじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="kanko">[https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/shiitorijinja/ 椎取神社] - Miyakejima Tourism Association</ref><ref name="official">[https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ 三宅島の神社 (富賀神社・御笏神社・椎取神社 他)]</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Shiritagi-no-Mikoto (しりたきのみこと) - identified as "Shitai," the fifth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="official"/>.

=== Former shrine building ===
Just south of the current torii and shrine building are the old torii and old shrine building, but they were buried by mudflow during the {{ill|lt=Miyake-jima#2000 eruption|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|lt=2000 eruption (Heisei 12)|1=Miyake-jima}}, so the present torii and shrine building were constructed just to the north<ref name="kanko"/>.

<gallery>
File:Buried Torii of Shiitori Shrie.jpg|Buried old torii and shrine building of "Shiitori Shrine"
</gallery>
